Test Scenarios: US10. As a coach, I can book a session for a client in the Workspace via "Book Session" CTA so that I can schedule a session with client
Open Book a session popup via CTA button and verify default fields
Step:
1. Navigate to the Calendar page with Booking feature enabled.
2. Click the "Book a session" CTA button.
3. Verify the "Book a session" popup appears.
4. Verify the background calendar is dimmed.
5. Verify focus is trapped inside the modal.
6. Verify all fields are present with correct defaults.
Test Data:
Logged in as Owner/Admin.
Booking feature enabled.
Current date: today.
Expected:
- "Book a session" popup opens.
- Background calendar is dimmed.
- Focus is trapped inside the modal.
- Popup title is "Book a session".
- Trainer field is disabled and shows WS Owner.
- Start Date defaults to today.
- Start Time defaults to 7:00 AM.
- Location defaults to "In-person".
- Duration defaults to 30 min.
- Fields present: Client (required), Start Date (required), Start Time (required), Type (optional), Name (required), Duration (required), Location (required), Description (optional), Add note to client (optional).
- CTA buttons "Book Session" and "Cancel" are visible.
- Close (x) icon is visible.

Test Scenarios: Open popup by clicking a time slot on Day/Week view and verify date/time prefill
Step:
1. Navigate to Calendar page on Day view.
2. Click the 6:00 AM–7:00 AM time slot.
3. Verify "Book a session" popup opens.
4. Verify prefilled Start Date and Start Time.
5. Switch to Week view.
6. Click the 9:00 AM–10:00 AM slot on a future date.
7. Verify prefilled Start Date and Start Time.
Test Data:
Logged in as Owner.
Day view: click on 6 AM slot.
Week view: click on 9 AM slot on next Monday.
Expected:
- Day view: popup opens with Start Date = clicked date, Start Time = 6:00 AM.
- Week view: popup opens with Start Date = clicked date, Start Time = 9:00 AM.
- All other defaults unchanged.

Test Scenarios: Open popup by clicking a time slot on Month view and verify date prefill
Step:
1. Navigate to Calendar page on Month view.
2. Click on a date cell (e.g., March 15).
3. Verify "Book a session" popup opens.
4. Verify prefilled Start Date.
Test Data:
Logged in as Owner.
Month view: click on March 15.
Expected:
- Popup opens with Start Date = March 15.
- Start Time defaults to 7:00 AM (no time prefill from Month view click).
- All other defaults unchanged.

Test Scenarios: Client field — open dropdown, display clients, scroll pagination
Step:
1. Open "Book a session" popup.
2. Click the Client field.
3. Verify dropdown opens with client list.
4. Verify excluded statuses are absent.
5. Verify sorting is alphabetical.
6. Scroll to the bottom of the initial 20 results.
7. Verify the next 20 clients load.
Test Data: WS has >20 clients including some with Pending, Archived, and Waiting Activation status.
Expected:
- Dropdown opens with searchable client list.
- Clients with Pending, Archived, or Waiting Activation status are NOT shown.
- Clients are sorted alphabetically by name.
- First 20 clients load initially.
- Scrolling loads the next 20 clients.
- Each client item shows: Profile image, Client name, Client email.

Test Scenarios: Client field — search by name, search by email, and no results
Step:
1. Open "Book a session" popup and click Client field.
2. Type a partial client name (e.g., "Joh").
3. Verify search triggers after ~200ms and filters list.
4. Clear input, type a partial client email (e.g., "john@").
5. Verify filtered results by email.
6. Clear input, type "zzznomatch999".
7. Verify no-result state.
Test Data:
Search by name: "Joh" (matches John Doe).
Search by email: "john@" (matches john@example.com).
No result query: "zzznomatch999".
Expected:
- After typing name query, list updates after ~200ms showing only matching clients.
- After typing email query, list updates showing matching clients.
- Selecting a client closes the dropdown.
- When no clients match, dropdown shows "No clients found." message.
- Dropdown list is blank (no client items) on no-result state.

Test Scenarios: Start Date field — keyboard input with valid and invalid month values
Step:
1. Open popup and click Start Date field.
2. Verify field value is highlighted (selected).
3. Type "Mar" — verify calendar picker hovers on Mar 1 of current year.
4. Type "Mar 15" — verify calendar hovers on Mar 15 current year.
5. Type "Mar 15, 2027" — verify calendar hovers on Mar 15, 2027.
6. Type invalid month "Xyz" — verify calendar keeps previous hover date.
7. Press Enter — verify date is applied and picker closes.
Test Data:
Keyboard inputs: "Mar", "Mar 15", "Mar 15, 2027", "Xyz".
WS created in 2022; current year is 2026.
Expected:
- Clicking Start Date highlights the current value.
- Typing "Mar" → calendar picker hovers on Mar 1, current year.
- Typing "Mar 15" → calendar hovers on Mar 15, current year.
- Typing "Mar 15, 2027" → calendar hovers on Mar 15, 2027.
- Typing invalid month "Xyz" → calendar keeps previous hover date.
- Pressing Enter applies the input value and closes the calendar picker.

Test Scenarios: Start Date field — date picker open, out-of-range date hover shows stop icon
Step:
1. Open popup and click Start Date field.
2. Verify the calendar picker opens.
3. Verify the currently selected date is visually highlighted.
4. Hover over a date before the WS creation date.
5. Verify the stop icon appears.
6. Hover over a date more than 1 year from today.
7. Verify the stop icon appears.
8. Select an in-range date.
9. Verify date is applied.
Test Data:
WS created in 2022.
Out-of-range dates: 2021-01-01 and {today + 2 years}.
Expected:
- Calendar picker opens when clicking Start Date.
- Currently selected date is visually highlighted.
- Hovering over a date before WS creation date shows a "stop" icon.
- Hovering over a date >1 year from today shows a "stop" icon.
- Selecting an in-range date applies it and closes the picker.
- Past, present, and future in-range dates are all selectable.

Test Scenarios: Start Time field — keyboard input and dropdown with timezone display
Step:
1. Open popup and click Start Time field.
2. Verify field value is highlighted and coach's timezone is shown.
3. Verify dropdown displays time slots in 15-minute increments.
4. Type "09:30 AM" — verify matching option is highlighted in dropdown.
5. Press Enter — verify time is applied and dropdown closes.
6. Click outside the dropdown — verify dropdown closes and value is applied.
Test Data:
Coach timezone: UTC+7.
Typed value: "09:30 AM".
Expected:
- Clicking Start Time highlights the current value.
- Coach's timezone (e.g., UTC+7) is displayed in the field.
- Dropdown shows time slots in 15-minute increments (12:00 AM, 12:15 AM, ...).
- Typing "09:30 AM" highlights 9:30 AM option in the dropdown (hover state).
- Pressing Enter applies "09:30 AM" and closes the dropdown.
- Clicking outside applies value and closes dropdown.

Test Scenarios: Add note to client — counter at 450 chars, input blocked at 500 chars
Step:
1. Open popup and click "Add note to client" button.
2. Verify textbox appears with placeholder "Add note to client" and cursor is focused.
3. Type 449 characters — verify no counter shown.
4. Type 1 more character (total 450) — verify character counter appears.
5. Continue typing to reach 500 characters — verify input is blocked at 500.
6. Attempt to type beyond 500 — verify no additional character is accepted.
Test Data: 449-char string, then add 1 char (450), then fill to 500 chars.
Expected:
- Clicking "Add note to client" shows the textbox with placeholder text and focused cursor.
- No counter displayed below 450 characters.
- Counter appears at 450 characters (e.g., "450/500").
- Input is blocked at 500 characters; no further characters accepted.

Test Scenarios: Type field — hover and click Edit icon on selected session type, apply custom changes

7. Verify "Apply" button is enabled.
8. Click "Apply".
9. Verify the popup closes and summary section reflects new Duration.

Expected:
- Hovering Edit icon shows tooltip "Change duration, location or description".

- Apply button is disabled by default.
- Changing duration to 90 min enables Apply button.
- Clicking Apply closes the edit popup, returns to Book Session popup.

Test Scenarios: Book session — future session with no overlap: success flow

Test Scenarios: Book session — future session with overlap detected and Book Anyway
Step:

3. Verify "Session Overlap" popup appears.
4. Verify description text and actions.
5. Click "Book Anyway".
6. Verify session is saved.
Test Data:
Overlapping session exists from 10:00 AM–10:30 AM.
New session: 10:00 AM–10:30 AM.
Expected:
- "Session Overlap" popup appears with title "Session Overlap".
- Description: "You already have another session during this time. Are you sure you want to book this session anyway?"
- Two actions: "Cancel" and "Book Anyway" (focused).
- Clicking "Book Anyway" saves the session, closes popup, shows success toast, renders session on calendar.

Test Scenarios: Book session — overlap popup: Cancel/X dismisses and returns with data persisted

Expected:

Test Scenarios: Book session — past date/time: skip overlap, show Past date confirmation popup
Step:

3. Verify no overlap check is triggered.
4. Verify "Session Date Is In the Past" popup appears.
5. Verify popup text and actions.
6. Click "Book Anyway".
7. Verify session is saved as Completed.

Expected:
- No overlap check triggered for past sessions.
- "Session Date Is In the Past" popup appears.
- Message: "You selected a Date and time that is in the past. This session will be saved as Completed. No client communication will be sent. Are you sure you want to book this session anyway?"
- Two actions: "Cancel" and "Book Anyway" (focused).
- Clicking "Book Anyway" saves session, closes popup, shows success toast.

Test Scenarios: Book session — past date popup: Cancel/X returns with data persisted
Step:

2. Click "Cancel".
3. Verify confirmation modal closes and Book a session popup is restored with data intact.

Expected:

Test Scenarios: Book session — API failure: popup stays open with data persisted and retry enabled
Step:

Expected:
- "Book a session" popup remains open.
- All coach's input data is preserved on the popup.
- "Book Session" CTA remains enabled for retry.
- Toast message "Failed to add a session. Please try again" is displayed.

Expected:

Test Scenarios: Cancel/close with unsaved changes — choose to Stay keeps popup open with data
Step:

2. Observe the calendar view.

Test Data: WS with existing sessions. Start on Week view.
Expected:
- Calendar page shows the calendar with all sessions from the WS.
- Sessions render according to the selected view (Day/Week/Month).

Test Scenarios: Empty state — no sessions in selected date range
Step:
1. Navigate to a date range with no sessions (e.g., far future date).
2. Observe the calendar.
3. Verify empty state behavior.
Test Data: Date range with no sessions (e.g., December 2030).
Expected:
- Empty calendar grid is displayed with no session cards or popovers.
- Calendar navigation and view-switching remain fully functional.

Test Scenarios: Day view — session block height and content display by duration (≤15min, ≤45min, 46-59min, ≥60min)
Step:
1. Navigate to Day view with sessions of durations: 10 min, 30 min, 50 min, 90 min.
2. Verify block height for the 10-min session.
3. Verify content display (1 line: client name, start time, session name) for 10-min session.
4. Verify block height for 30-min session and content (1 line).
5. Verify block height for 50-min session and content (2 lines: line 1 = client + start time; line 2 = session name).
6. Verify block height for 90-min session and content (3 lines: line 1 = client; line 2 = start–end time; line 3 = session name).
Test Data: Sessions: 10 min (fixed 1/4 slot height), 30 min (1/2 slot), 50 min (50/60 slot), 90 min (1.5 slots).
Expected:
- 10-min session: block = 1/4 of 1-hour slot, 1 line (client name, start time, session name).
- 30-min session: block = 1/2 slot, 1 line display.
- 50-min session: block = 50/60 slot, 2-line display (client + time on line 1, session name on line 2).
- 90-min session: block = 1.5 slots, 3-line display (client; start–end time; session name).
- Long content is truncated within the block.

Test Scenarios: Day view — session time display format (same am/pm vs different am/pm)

Session A: 10:00 AM–11:30 AM (same am/pm).

- Session A: Line 2 shows "10:00 AM – 11:30 AM" (both am/pm displayed).
- Session B: Line 2 shows only the end am/pm (e.g., "11:00 PM – 1:00 AM").

Test Scenarios: Day view — horizontal stacking and dynamic width for overlapping sessions
Step:
1. Navigate to Day view with 2 fully overlapping sessions (same start/end time).
2. Verify sessions are displayed side by side.
3. Verify each session's width is reduced proportionally.
4. Add a 3rd partially overlapping session.
5. Verify all 3 are stacked horizontally and width adjusts.
6. Verify sort order: earlier start time on left; same start time → longer duration on left; same start+duration → older created_at on left.
Test Data:
Session A: 10:00 AM–11:00 AM, created first.
Session B: 10:00 AM–11:00 AM, created second.
Session C: 10:15 AM–10:45 AM.
Expected:
- Session A and B display side by side with each at ~50% width.
- Session C is stacked horizontally with A and B; all widths adjust proportionally.
- Sort order: A (left), B (middle), C (right) based on start time/duration/created_at.
- Sessions shift left to fill vacant slots.

Cross-day session: Jan 30 11:00 PM – Feb 1 1:00 AM.
Overlapping sessions on the same day.
